entries.title: 2.2L Petrol

compatibleVariants: 2.2 VTEC, 2.2 VTEC Type S, SiR (JDM import)
commonFailures: VTEC solenoid failure causing P1259 code (60k-100k miles)
Timing belt tensioner wear leading to interference damage
Oil consumption from piston ring wear on high-mileage examples

compatibleVariants: 2.2 VTEC, Type SH, Type SH ATTS
commonFailures: VTEC solenoid clogging from oil sludge (70k+ miles)

commonProblems.h2: Honda Prelude Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: VTEC Solenoid Failure - Why H22A Engines Lose Variable Timing
problems.affectedModels: Honda Prelude 2.2 VTEC, Type SH, SiR (1992-2001, H22A1 & H22A4)
problems.rootCause: Oil sludge buildup clogs the VTEC solenoid screen, preventing oil pressure from engaging the variable valve timing mechanism, triggering P1259 codes and reduced performance.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove VTEC solenoid, clean screen or replace unit, reset ECU codes
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early before internal damage; may recur if oil maintenance poor

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace solenoid, VTC actuator, timing components, flush oil galleries
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses multiple wear points; suitable for engines with good compression

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded solenoid, timing kit, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage engines; includes 12-month warranty and upgraded components

problems.recommendation: If your Prelude shows P1259 codes but still runs smoothly, a solenoid clean or replacement may suffice. However, if the engine has over 80k miles or shows oil consumption, a rebuilt H22A with upgraded VTEC components offers better long-term value and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt H22A engine prices for your Honda Prelude

problems.h4: Timing Belt Tensioner Wear - Preventing Catastrophic Interference Damage
problems.affectedModels: Honda Prelude 2.2 VTEC, 2.2i, Type SH (1992-2001, H22A & H23A)
problems.rootCause: The hydraulic tensioner loses pressure over time, allowing belt slack that can cause timing jump and piston-to-valve contact on these interference engines.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idlers, water pump per Honda TSB
repairOptions.longevity: Essential preventative maintenance; recommended every 60k miles or 5 years

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removal, valve replacement, guide seals, reassembly
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if block and pistons undamaged; labour-intensive with uncertain outcome

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine Supply & Fit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with new timing kit, bench-tested, warranty included
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution for jumped timing; eliminates hidden damage risk

problems.recommendation: Never ignore timing belt service intervals on the Prelude. If the belt has jumped, replacement is almost always more cost-effective than repair due to the risk of hidden damage. A rebuilt engine with upgraded tensioner components offers the safest path forward.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Honda Prelude timing belt replacement or engine supply

problems.h4: Oil Consumption Problem - High-Mileage H22/H23 Piston Ring Wear
problems.affectedModels: Honda Prelude 2.2i, 2.2 VTEC, Type SH (1992-2001, H22A & H23A)
problems.rootCause: Piston ring wear and valve stem seal deterioration allow oil to enter combustion chambers, causing blue smoke, fouled spark plugs, and eventual catalytic converter damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace PCV valve, valve cover gasket, spark plug tube seals
repairOptions.longevity: May reduce minor seepage; won't fix worn rings or major consumption

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removal, valve stem seals, decarbonise, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses valve-side oil loss; piston rings remain original wear items

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Rings
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with upgraded piston rings, oil control system, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best solution for high-mileage consumption; addresses root cause with warranty protection

problems.recommendation: If your Prelude burns more than 1 litre per 1,000 miles, minor fixes won't resolve the underlying ring wear. A rebuilt engine with upgraded oil control components offers reliable performance and protects your investment in this enthusiast classic.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt H23A engine prices for your Honda Prelude 2.2i

problems.h4: Distributor Ignition Failure - No-Start Conditions on Early Preludes
problems.rootCause: Age-related deterioration of the distributor internal ignition module and cap/rotor causes intermittent spark loss, rough idle, or complete no-start conditions.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cap, rotor, inspect leads; reset ignition timing
repairOptions.longevity: Quick fix for misfires; won't resolve failed internal ignition module

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace complete distributor assembly with new ignition module
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable solution for B-series ignition issues; genuine or quality aftermarket parts

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt early-gen engine with updated ignition components
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage B-series engines; includes warranty and compatibility verification

problems.recommendation: For early Preludes showing ignition issues, start with a full distributor replacement. If the engine has high mileage or other wear signs, a rebuilt B20A/B21A1 offers better long-term value and preserves your classic Prelude's usability.
problems.cta: Check rebuilt B20A engine availability for your Honda Prelude 2.0 Si

problems.h4: Head Gasket Failure - Overheating Damage on High-Mileage Preludes
problems.affectedModels: Honda Prelude 2.2 VTEC, 2.2i, Type SH (1992-2001, H22A & H23A)
problems.rootCause: Coolant system neglect, overheating events, or age-related gasket deterioration allows coolant/oil mixing, compression loss, and potential block warping.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace gasket, resurface if needed, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if block/head undamaged; requires thorough cooling system inspection

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head pressure test, valve job, new gasket, full cooling system overhaul
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses common warping; still risks hidden block damage on severe overheating

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine Supply & Fit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with new gaskets, cooling components, bench-tested, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Safest solution for overheated engines; eliminates uncertainty with warranty protection

problems.recommendation: Head gasket failure on a high-mileage Prelude often indicates broader cooling system neglect. Unless the engine has very low miles and a documented service history, replacement with a rebuilt unit offers more predictable costs and long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Honda Prelude head gasket repair or engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Honda Prelude
finalCta.paragraph: The Honda Prelude's enthusiast appeal means replacement decisions matter. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Honda Prelude specialists who understand H22 VTEC calibration, JDM import compliance, and manual transmission compatibility. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing known failure points, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and keep your Prelude on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Honda Prelude Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Honda Prelude and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Honda Prelude Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Honda Prelude's 1987-2001 UK production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their H22A/H23A engine and compare rebuilt prices.

years.description: The second-generation Prelude introduced fuel-injected B20A and B21A1 engines, establishing the model's reputation for reliable, rev-happy performance.
keyChanges: Introduction of fuel injection on B21A1 (1988)
Updated chassis codes BA4-BA9 for UK market compliance
Carburetted B20A phased out in favour of EFI systems
knownFor: Distributor ignition module failures causing no-start conditions
Carburettor jet clogging on early B20A models
Reliable daily drivers with simple maintenance requirements
years.cta: Get quotes for 1987-1991 Honda Prelude replacement engines

years.description: The golden era began with the H22A VTEC engine, delivering 190hp and variable valve timing that defined the Prelude's performance legacy.
keyChanges: Introduction of H22A VTEC engine with variable valve timing
New BB1-BB4 chassis with improved handling dynamics
H23A non-VTEC variant offered as lower-cost alternative
mainEngines: H22A1 - 2.2L VTEC petrol (2.2 VTEC, SiR JDM)
knownFor: VTEC solenoid failure triggering P1259 codes (60k-100k miles)
Timing belt tensioner wear requiring preventative replacement
Strong enthusiast following for SiR JDM import models

faq.h2: Honda Prelude Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Honda Prelude engine replacement cost?
items.answer: Honda Prelude engine replacement costs range from £595 for used H23A units to £2,595 for rebuilt H22A VTEC engines, supply only. Prices vary by variant, engine code, mileage history and whether VTEC components are upgraded.
keyPoints: Used H23A non-VTEC engines start around £595-£1,495
Rebuilt H22A VTEC engines typically £1,295-£2,595 supply only
Supply & fit labour adds £450-£850 depending on garage location
VTEC solenoid upgrades may increase cost but improve reliability
items.cta: Compare live Honda Prelude engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: What engine codes fit my Honda Prelude?
items.answer: Honda Preludes use H22A1, H22A4, H23A1, B20A or B21A1 engine codes depending on generation and trim. Enter your registration above to instantly verify compatibility with VTEC/non-VTEC configurations and manual/auto transmissions.
keyPoints: H22A1/H22A4: 2.2L VTEC for 1992-2001 2.2 VTEC/Type SH models
H23A1: 2.2L non-VTEC for 1992-2001 2.2i variants
B20A/B21A1: 2.0-2.1L for 1987-1991 second-generation models
JDM SiR imports require H22A1 with VTEC calibration verification
items.cta: Use our free VIN checker to confirm your Honda Prelude engine code

items.question: How long does Honda Prelude engine replacement take?
items.answer: Most Honda Prelude engine replacements take 2-4 working days at specialist UK garages, depending on variant complexity, VTEC calibration requirements and parts availability for your specific H22A/H23A engine code.
keyPoints: Standard H23A non-VTEC swaps typically complete in 2-3 days
H22A VTEC engines may require 3-4 days for calibration checks
JDM SiR imports need additional time for ECU compatibility verification
Supply-only engines can be dispatched within 24-48 hours from stock
items.cta: Book Honda Prelude supply & fit with approved UK specialists

items.question: What warranty comes with Honda Prelude replacement engines?
items.answer: All Honda Prelude replacement engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering VTEC solenoid, timing components and major internal parts from UK specialist rebuilders.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across network
Coverage includes VTEC solenoid, timing kit and major components
Some specialists offer extended 24-month options for rebuilt units
Warranty terms confirmed with chosen supplier before order placement
items.cta: View full Honda Prelude engine warranty details before comparing quotes

items.question: Can I upgrade my Honda Prelude engine size?
items.answer: Honda Prelude engine upgrades from B20A to H22A are possible but require ECU, wiring harness and transmission compatibility verification. Consult a Honda specialist before ordering to ensure VTEC calibration matches your chassis and intended use.
keyPoints: B20A to H22A swaps need ECU and wiring harness adaptation
Manual transmission models require clutch fork compatibility checks
JDM H22A imports need UK emissions calibration verification
ATTS-equipped Type SH models require system-specific calibration
items.cta: Consult Honda specialists about Prelude engine upgrade compatibility

items.question: What are common Honda Prelude engine problems?
items.answer: Common Honda Prelude engine problems include VTEC solenoid failure (P1259 code), timing belt tensioner wear, and oil consumption on high-mileage H22A/H23A engines. Rebuilt units often include upgraded components addressing these known issues.
keyPoints: VTEC solenoid clogging triggers P1259 codes on H22A engines
Timing belt tensioner wear risks interference damage if neglected
Oil consumption from piston ring wear common above 90k miles
Rebuilt engines often include upgraded solenoid and timing components
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Honda Prelude engines with failure-mitigation upgrades

items.question: Is a rebuilt Honda Prelude engine better than used?
items.answer: Rebuilt Honda Prelude engines typically offer better long-term value than used units, with upgraded VTEC solenoids, timing components and piston rings addressing known failure points, plus warranty protection for peace of mind.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines include upgraded components targeting known failures
Compression testing and bench dyno verification before dispatch
12-month warranty standard vs limited coverage on used units
Used engines require careful compression and history verification
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs used Honda Prelude engine value propositions

items.question: How do I find my Honda Prelude engine code?
items.answer: Your Honda Prelude engine code is stamped on the engine block near the cylinder head, visible on the driver's side, or listed on the vehicle's VIN plate under the bonnet. Enter your registration above for instant code verification.
keyPoints: Engine code stamped on block near cylinder head (driver's side)
VIN plate under bonnet lists chassis and engine specifications
Service history documents often record original engine code
Online VIN decoders can cross-reference chassis to H22A/H23A codes
items.cta: Find your Honda Prelude engine code with our free registration checker

items.question: Does Honda Prelude engine replacement affect insurance?
items.answer: Most UK insurers accept Honda Prelude engine replacements with like-for-like H22A/H23A codes, but you must notify your provider and supply documentation confirming VTEC calibration and emissions compliance for MOT validity.
keyPoints: Notify insurer before replacement to confirm policy acceptance
Provide documentation of engine code and VTEC calibration match
Like-for-like H22A/H23A swaps rarely affect premium calculations
Keep replacement invoice and warranty documents for claims purposes
items.cta: Confirm insurance requirements before booking Honda Prelude engine work

items.question: Can I get supply and fit for my Honda Prelude engine?
items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket's network includes UK Honda specialists offering full supply & fit services for Honda Prelude H22A/H23A engines, with VTEC calibration verification, manual/auto transmission compatibility checks and post-install testing included.
keyPoints: Approved Honda specialists handle VTEC calibration and ECU matching
Manual transmission models receive clutch fork compatibility verification
Post-install road test and ECU code clearance included in service
Nationwide delivery and fitting available from specialist garages
